title: Is Clockiel really Matariel Also Trivia
display_title: Is Clockiel really Matariel Also Trivia
wikitext:
[]: Is Clockiel really Matariel? (Also, Trivia.)[SPOILERS]             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Sun Sep 06, 2009 1:21 pm It would make sense. They both come from water on stilt like legs. They both are the 2nd largest Angels in their continuity. The both die in 5 seconds. (BTW, Misato's cell phone sounds exactly like King Ghidorah's bell like cry...)

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Sep 06, 2009 1:24 pm Clocky is Matariel's replacement. It was also a lot stronger but still weak.

[]:             Quote Post by Dranzer » Sun Sep 06, 2009 2:45 pm Well we can say that.BTW what is he using against Unit 02?Pales?...

[]: Re: Is Clockiel really Matariel? (Also, Trivia.)[SPOILERS]             Quote Post by DevilzillA » Sun Sep 06, 2009 6:18 pm CyberXIII wrote: (BTW, Misato's cell phone sounds exactly like King Ghidorah's bell like cry...)

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sun Sep 06, 2009 6:25 pm I think we can all say that clocky is a combo angel.It uses original attacks that are much more effective then what spidey used in the series.And has the same short legs and comes over the ocean.However,they both come from different directions though.Clocky comes from the left side and spidey comes from the right.Other then that,Clocky is definently a combo with it and spidey. And I realize spidey isnt the actual name but I perfer it since it looks like a spider.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Sun Sep 06, 2009 9:23 pm They are kinda useless Angels...Just put there to be a way to show off the new character. Not a lot is needed to kill them, they may have looked cool but a better battle would have been nice.

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Mon Sep 07, 2009 12:47 am Ive had a new theory. Angel fights will be short when it's an introductory fight only. It doesn't seem like any new unknown characters are being brought in 3.0 & 4.0 so I think the fights would be substantially better in the upcoming events.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Mon Sep 07, 2009 1:02 am You never truly know anymore..So don't hold your breath. Anything could happen.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Sep 07, 2009 1:06 am Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: don't hold your breath. Anything could happen.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Mon Sep 07, 2009 1:49 am wonderluster wrote: Ive had a new theory. Angel fights will be short when it's an introductory fight only. It doesn't seem like any new unknown characters are being brought in 3.0 & 4.0 so I think the fights would be substantially better in the upcoming events.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Sep 07, 2009 2:43 am I'm pretty damn certain the first 15 minutes of 3.0 is going to go down in some form like this: 2.0 ends with all their Evas (and pilots) out of commission. An Angel shows up and despite their misgivings, NERV and Gendo give the go ahead for Kaworu and his Mystery Eva 06 to take the Angel down. He kicks its ass swiftly, giving his fans the epic action hero moment they've always wanted him to have.

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Mon Sep 07, 2009 10:42 am I think Clocky is a ricer angel.

[]: Re: Is Clockiel really Matariel? (Also, Trivia.)[SPOILERS]             Quote Post by LiLi » Mon Sep 07, 2009 11:56 am DevilzillA wrote: CyberXIII wrote: (BTW, Misato's cell phone sounds exactly like King Ghidorah's bell like cry...)

[]:             Quote Post by DevilzillA » Mon Sep 07, 2009 12:17 pm Eiji Tsuburaya was a Catholic, hence Mothra's symbol being a cross. In one of his Ultraman shows, all of the Ultramen were crucified... http://farm4.static.flickr.com/3211/2727838406_1f73cfb07a.jpg Tokusatsu references seemed more prevalent in Nadia, where the Nautilus is directly based on the Alpha from "Latitude Zero", not mention its hull being constructed of space titanium ("Godzilla vs. Mechagodzilla"), or the Atlanteans themselves being from Ultraman's homeworld, Nebula M-78...

[]:             Quote Post by Dranzer » Mon Sep 07, 2009 12:19 pm It wasn't a very useless Angel... without "F" type equipment,Evas would have a problem to defeat it...Because they couldn't get close enough to the core.(Ya know,the "Feint" core..." but after that,they would have an easy job.)

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Mon Sep 07, 2009 7:59 pm Wasn't Rei and Unit 00's weapon of choice a sniper rifle?

[]:             Quote Post by Dranzer » Mon Sep 07, 2009 11:17 pm wonderluster wrote: Wasn't Rei and Unit 00's weapon of choice a sniper rifle?

[]:             Quote Post by LIwarriorTKD » Tue Sep 08, 2009 1:47 am Anno def references Ultraman throughout his work. If I recall correctly I'm pretty sure there's a portion of Shiki-Jitsu where his characters speak about Ultraman. On-Topic: It def seems like Clockiel is drawing reference from Matariel. I also really agree with Gendo's Papa, it would def seem appropriate at this point to throw us for a loop to start 3.0 off with a crazy Kaworu fight... o man these movies need to come out soon... hmmmm

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Tue Sep 08, 2009 8:23 am ..weak Adams.. I do believe it is a "form" of the Angel thought, could be a combination of two or just a redrawing of it.

[]:             Quote Post by sean_sean_sean_sean » Tue Sep 08, 2009 10:33 am I'm pretty damn certain the first 15 minutes of 3.0 is going to go down in some form like this: 2.0 ends with all their Evas (and pilots) out of commission. An Angel shows up and despite their misgivings, NERV and Gendo give the go ahead for Kaworu and his Mystery Eva 06 to take the Angel down. He kicks its ass swiftly, giving his fans the epic action hero moment they've always wanted him to have.
